fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Missouri Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Missouri - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity missouri, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to missouri, respiratory therapist compact missouri, missouri respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Missouri Board for Respiratory Care
board.mailing_address: 3605 Missouri Boulevard, P.O. Box 1335, Jefferson City, MO 65102-1335

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Missouri.

steps.title: Create an Account
steps.description: Navigate to the Missouri Division of Professional Registration Online Application System and click "First Time User? Register Now" to create your applicant profile.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Log into your account and select the "Respiratory Care Practitioner" application type. Thoroughly complete all required sections, including personal, educational, and professional background details.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documents
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your COARC-accredited program and official NBRC score reports (CRT/RRT) to be sent directly to the Missouri Board of Respiratory Care.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check (CBC)
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided within the online application to complete the fingerprint-based criminal background check through the state's approved vendor, MACHS.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $125 via the secure payment portal within the Missouri Division of Professional Registration Online Application System.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check your application status within the Online Application System. The Board will notify you of any missing documentation or additional requirements.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Missouri respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Missouri have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Missouri. To work in Missouri, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Missouri?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Missouri while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Missouri
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Therapist license in Missouri, use the Missouri Division of Professional Registration Online Application System, which handles both initial and endorsement applications for Respiratory Care Practitioners.
